conjecture_title: Informal Conjecture (uniform twin-width strictly stronger than twin-width)
summary: The conjecture that uniform twin-width is strictly stronger than twin-width — i.e., that there exist groups with finite twin-width but infinite uniform twin-width — remains open as of 2026. The group of finitely supported permutations on \(\mathbb{Z}\) is proposed in the source paper as a candidate witness, but its uniform twin-width status has not been determined. A wide search of the literature citing arXiv:2204.12330 found no follow-up paper claiming progress on this specific question.
search_queries: uniform twin-width groups finite infinite Bonnet Geniet Tessera Thomassé arXiv 2204.12330
notes: Semantic Scholar lists ~20 papers citing arXiv:2204.12330; none found to address the uniform-vs-plain twin-width separation. The Poisson-boundary paper on lampshuffler groups (arXiv:2307.08878) cites the source paper but does not discuss twin-width. The conjecture is approximately 4 years old and no resolution was detected.
